violations.text: The following was observed: 1) Various items in the flip top prep unit near the batter station was observed at 45-52 degrees Fahrenheit. 2) Various items in both the cold drawers under the cookline was observed between 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Per PIC, all raw meats and seafood was placed in the unit under 2 hours prior to inspection. All other items were kept in the units overnight. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of items at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2025

Reduced oxygen packaged tuna and mahi mahi was observed in refrigeration with the package in-tact. Fish was observed between 41-49 degrees Fahrenheit. Reduced oxygen packaged seafood must be removed from the bag upon thawing. Person in charge disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/06/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Chicken and beef were observed held at 43-47 degrees Fahrenheit in cold-drawer #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the cold-drawer unit for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The initial ambient air temperature was observed held at 46 Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Cold-drawer unit was adjusted and the temperature dropped to 39 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Establishment must monitor unit. If cold-drawer unit cannot maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below it must be taken out of service and repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Sour cream was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit in the tabletop cold-hold unit #2. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Sour cream was observed stored over the fill line. Per person in charge, sour cream had been in the unit for less than 4 hours. Sour cream was relocated to another refrigeration unit to cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Cooked broccoli was observed held at 54 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Cooked broccoli was observed stored over the fill line. Per person in charge, broccoli had been placed there about an hour prior to inspection. Broccoli was relocated to another refrigeration unit to cool. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Food was observed held at 43-46 degrees Fahrenheit in prep unit #3. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the prep unit since the day prior. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Prep unit had an ambient air temperature of 46 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Prep unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Prep unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Lettuce was observed held at 46 degrees Fahrenheit in the tabletop cold-hold unit #1. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, lettuce had been in the unit for less than four hours. Lettuce was relocated to another refrigeration unit to cool. The ambient air temperature was observed at 48 degrees Fahrenheit at time of inspection. Cold-hold unit was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Cold-hold unit must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Pans of food were observed stacked on top of each other with the bottom of the pan touch the food below it. Establishment must not stack pans of food on top of each other to prevent contamination. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/13/2025

Sour cream, guacamole and other sauces were observed held at 48-50 degrees Fahrenheit in the countertop cold holding unit. Items were in portion cups and in hotel pans deeper than 4 inches. Staff to now put sauces and condiments directly into the hotel pans to allow for proper air-flow circulation. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Items were moved to the walk-in freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The cutting boards on the flip top prep unit next to the hot-holding unit and above the sink near the cookline are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The establishment is lacking a 3-bay sink in the main and banquet bar areas. A sink with at least 3 compartments shall be provided for manually washing, rinsing, and SANITIZING EQUIPMENT and UTENSILS in ware-washing areas (including bars). Person in charge to send proof 3-bay sink installation to inspector. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine (white guard) was observed with an accumulation of biofilm. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The prep sink was observed with an air-break. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
No person in charge certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

An open employees' beverage container was observed stored in a food preparation area. Employees shall not drink from open beverage containers in order to prevent contamination of their hands, the container, and exposed food. Cups were removed from food prep area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Shellstock tags did not have all of the required information. The last date of sale were lacking and were not stored in chronological order. Shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Raw chicken was observed thawing above raw shrimp in the walk-in cooler. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knife) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Equipment was moved to the warewashing area to be washed, rinsed, and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Commercially manufactured mashed potatoes were observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit in the front hot-holding unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Mashed potatoes were reheated above 135 at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

1) Sour cream was observed held at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the countertop cold-hold unit that was not operating at the time of inspection. 2) Pasta was observed at 50 degrees Fahrenheit in the flip top prep unit. The pasta was observed to be stored over the fill line. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per PIC, the items were placed in the unit 2.5 hours prior to inspection and was moved to the freezer to rapidly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Ribs and sausage gravy were placed hot in the refrigerator in containers greater than four inches deep. Items were moved into shallow pans to properly cool. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The following was observed: 1) Chicken was observed thawing on a speed rack. Person in charge moved chicken to the food prep sink under running cold water. 2) Reduced oxygen packaged lobster was observed thawing with the packaging in-tact at 41 degrees Fahrenheit. Person in charge voluntarily disposed of food at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The following was observed: 1) Tongs were observed stored on the handle of the oven 2) The ice scoop was observed stored on an unclean surface (on top of ice machine) Utensils were moved to the proper location by person in charge. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The following was observed: 1) The gasket of the hot-holding unit next to the cook/grill line was observed with a loose gasket. 2) The top of the flip top prep unit next to the breading station was observed to be loose New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The cutting boards on the flip top prep unit next to the hot-holding unit and above the sink near the cookline are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The establishment is lacking a 3-bay sink in the main and banquet bar areas. A sink with at least 3 compartments shall be provided for manually washing, rinsing, and SANITIZING EQUIPMENT and UTENSILS in ware-washing areas (including bars). Person in charge to send proof 3-bay sink installation to inspector. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023

The following was observed: 1) The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine (white guard) was observed with an accumulation of biofilm. 2) The wires above to cookline was observed with an accumulation of dust3) The mixer was observed with an accumulation of food debris New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The prep sink was observed with an air-break. A plumbing system shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply by providing an air gap. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024
The condensate from the walk-in freezer unit was observed to accumulate an excess amount of ice above the fan covers. New Violation. Correct By: 04/10/2024

The floors and walls throughout the facility (including floors in walk-in freezer) have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the clean equipment storage area. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/27/2024

The establishment is lacking a 3-bay sink in the main and banquet bar areas. A sink with at least 3 compartments shall be provided for manually washing, rinsing, and SANITIZING EQUIPMENT and UTENSILS in ware-washing areas (including bars). Person in charge to send proof 3-bay sink installation to inspector. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer, knives, and plates under the serving area in the kitchen) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Beets observed without date-marking in the cookline prep unit. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Sirloin steak can be served raw or undercooked to the customers request. The item is missing an asterisk on the menu to disclose that it is raw or undercooked. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the cookline refrigeration unit(s). Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 09/06/2023

The door to the outside, located in the outside dumpster area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The following were observed in poor repair: 1) The gaskets of the flip top prep units and the single door reach in near the prep sink 2) The top cover of the flip top prep units at the cookline observed with duct tape and not closing properly - PIC informed inspector that maintenance/new items ordered for repairs. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
The temperature measuring device for the dishwashing machine not working. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

The establishment is lacking a 3-bay sink in the main and banquet bar areas. A sink with at least 3 compartments shall be provided for manually washing, rinsing, and SANITIZING EQUIPMENT and UTENSILS in ware-washing areas (including bars). Person in charge to send proof 3-bay sink installation to inspector. Failure to comply may result in disciplinary action. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 12/01/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the (shelving in the server side of the cookline) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The ice machine in the kitchen was observed with an airbreak. An airgap shall be installed to prevent backflow of a liquid or gas contaminant into the water supply. New Violation. Correct By: 09/20/2023

The walls and floors in the cookline, ware-washing, food prep, and mop sink area near the walk-in refrigerator and freezer has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The Person in charge did not assure compliance with critical code requirements. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Soap was not available at the hand-washing sink in the kitchen. Hand-washing sinks must be provided with a supply of hand cleaning liquid, powder, or bar soap. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Hand sink observed being used as a dump sink in the warewashing and bar areas. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The following was observed of shellstock tags: 1) The shellstock in walk-in cooler lacking shellstock tags 2) The shellstock tags in the kitchen were not maintained in chronological order from the last day of sale 3) The shellstock tags did not have the last date of sale marked on the shellstock tags Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine, knives, plates in shelving under serving area in kitchen, robo-coop and strainers) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service at the time of inspection. Ice was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwashers was 0 parts per million (ppm) in the main bar area. Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. At the time of the inspection, maintenance service was preformed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/01/2023

The quaternary ammonium concentration in the sanitizing solution of the 3-bay sink and sanitizer buckets observed at 100 parts per million (ppm). According to the manufacturer's use directions, the concentration of the quaternary ammonium must be 150-400 ppm. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Beef and beer cheese sauce, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 48 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding in the hot holding unit. Hot-holding unit was turned on 1.5 hours prior to inspection and food was placed directly into unit before unit reached proper ambient temperature. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Food was reheated above 165 degrees Fahrenheit by staff. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Chicken, marinara sauce and jambalaya which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Food observed between 47-51 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in coolers. Food was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Marinara was observed held at 120 degrees Fahrenheit in hot-hold unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Staff reheated food above 165 degrees Fahrenheit. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The following was observed for improper cold holding of time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food 1) Various items observed held between 48-51 degrees Fahrenheit in both countertop prep units near the server stations. Refrigeration units were turned on at the time of inspection and items placed directly into unit before unit reached proper ambient temperature. Items moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. 2) Garlic and liquid eggs observed out of refrigeration in the prep area overnight. PIC voluntarily disposed of items. 3) Garlic butter observed held at 76 degrees Fahrenheit on the table near the walk-in cooler for 2 hours. Item moved to refrigeration to rapidly cool. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Various food items observed not marked with the date of preparation throughout the kitchens refrigeration units. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Prosciutto was observed with a date mark exceeding 7 days. Items prepped on-site shall be used within 7 days. Food was voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Sirloin steak can be served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. The item is missing an asterisk on the menu to disclose that it is raw or undercooked. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
Working containers used for storing chemicals (spray bottle with bleach) taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Chicken, jambalaya and marinara sauce were cooling overnight in containers deeper than 4 inches. Chicken observed in containers and covered. Cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged lobster that is labeled "keep at -18 degrees Fahrenheit and thaw in refrigeration immediately upon use" with the package intact. Lobster was observed at 37 degrees Fahrenheit and package was pierced open. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients (nuts, croutons, etc.) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

The door to the outside, located in the outside dumpster area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
Bags of pasta in the prep walk-in cooler observed to be splashed by possible beef juice or other food liquid. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Observed employees with soiled clothing that could present a possible contamination with food. Food may also be contaminated through direct contact with dirty clothing. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The following was observed improperly stored: 1) Containers being used as scoops in dry ingredients 2) Ice scoop observed on the top of the ice machine New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Tableware was observed to be displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
Observed employee touching lip contact surface of clean and sanitized tableware in the kitchen near the server side of the cookline. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The cutting board of the prep table near the prep sink was very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

The following were observed in poor repair: 1) The gaskets of the flip top prep units and the single door reach in near the prep sink 2) The top cover of the flip top prep units at the cookline observed with duct tape and not closing properly 3) A knife observed to be chipped in the knife rack4) Wire baskets observed to be frayed Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quatenary ammonium sanitizing solution for the 3-bay sink and sanitizer buckets. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023
The temperature measuring device for the dishwashing machine not working. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

The establishment is lacking a 3-bay sink in the main and banquet bar areas. A sink with at least 3 compartments shall be provided for manually washing, rinsing, and SANITIZING EQUIPMENT and UTENSILS in warewashing areas (including bars). New Violation. Correct By: 09/08/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the (shelving in the server side of the cookline) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The outside dumpster is not covered. Receptacles and waste handling units for refuse, recyclables, and returnables shall be kept covered. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
The walls near the main walk-in cooler area is in poor repair (warped). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/14/2023

The walls and floors in the cookline, ware-washing, food prep, and mop sink area near the walk-in refrigerator and freezer has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored in the clean equipment area near warewashing. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 08/31/2023
